metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge AC 5 into AC 3 (or explicitly split them) so it is unambiguous whether 'novel high-priority preserved' and 'carryover enforced' are sub-assertions of the single convergence test or independent test cases.
findings.finding: AC line 3 ('FakeRunner test: convergence on a remediation edit') and AC line 5 ('novel high-priority preserved; carryover still enforced at the normal bar') are near-duplicate requirements: the body already enumerates '(novel low-priority dropped, carryover enforced, novel high-priority preserved)' as sub-cases of the single convergence test, so AC 5 rechecks assertions already covered by AC 3, creating ambiguity about whether they represent one test or two independent tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC 3 as: 'FakeRunner convergence test in `tests/unit/` asserting: (a) novel low-priority finding is dropped, (b) novel high-priority finding is preserved, (c) carryover finding is still enforced at the normal bar.' Then remove AC 5 as redundant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the convergence and precondition-fallback test ACs require NEW tests beyond what ec89/cc5b already delivered (e.g. an end-to-end `_run_plan_review` integration test via injected FakeRunner that exercises the full wrapper, not just the individual seams) or explicitly call out the existing tests as the intended proof.
findings.finding: The FakeRunner convergence test and precondition-fallback tests described in the ACs already exist in the codebase as deliverables of the sibling tasks, creating ambiguity about whether this task's test ACs add new coverage or merely re-assert already-landed tests.
findings.location: AC lines: 'FakeRunner test: convergence on a remediation edit in tests/unit/' and 'FakeRunner tests: each precondition fallback -> byte-identical full review'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the AC: 'The convergence test is a new end-to-end test that drives `_run_plan_review` / `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` with a `FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})` injection, distinct from the seam-level tests already in test_plan_review_rising_floor.py and test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py (those are the sibling tasks' deliverables, not this task's).' This scopes the new test to the wrapper integration layer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: Confirm the ADR is created as `docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` to resolve the pre-existing hard-coded link in `docs/plan-review-gate.md:220`.
findings.finding: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (line 220) already hard-codes a link to `adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md`, but AC2 only says 'an ADR under `docs/adr/`' with no filename. If the ADR is created with any other name, the pre-existing cross-reference in `plan-review-gate.md` will be a dangling link — silently broken.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the required filename to AC2: 'An ADR at `docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md`' (matching the pre-existing cross-reference in `docs/plan-review-gate.md:220`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` is created as a deliverable of this task (it is cross-referenced from `docs/plan-review-gate.md:220` but does not yet exist in `docs/adr/`).
findings.finding: docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md does not exist in the repo, but docs/plan-review-gate.md (line 220) already contains a live cross-reference to it by exact name. The plan's AC2 requires creating this ADR — if it is not delivered, the docs/plan-review-gate.md link will be permanently broken.
findings.location: AC2 / docs/adr/ — plan requires 'An ADR under docs/adr/ records the design, 3 rejected alternatives, and the invariants'
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md with the standard ADR front-matter (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences) covering: the chosen design (separate Pass-2 novelty sub-call + deterministic Pass-3 floor), the three rejected alternatives (e.g. norm_id fingerprint-only, holistic LLM severity re-score, per-criterion skip list), and the invariants (Pass-1 unanchored; novelty in a SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call; drop decision deterministic in Pass-3).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Create `docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` recording the design, the three rejected alternatives (e.g. norm_id exact-match fingerprint, per-criterion anchoring, per-criterion skipping), and the three invariants (Pass-1 unanchored; novelty in a separate Pass-2 sub-call; drop decision deterministic in Pass-3) so the dangling cross-reference in `docs/plan-review-gate.md:220` resolves.
findings.finding: ADR 0007 is promised by AC ('An ADR under docs/adr/ records the design…') and already cross-referenced from the delivered docs/plan-review-gate.md:220 as 'ADR [0007](adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md)', but the file does not exist — docs/adr/ contains only 0001-0006. The plan will ship with a dangling reference in the existing doc section.
findings.location: AC: 'An ADR under docs/adr/ records the design, 3 rejected alternatives, and the invariants'; docs/plan-review-gate.md line 220
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/0007-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md with the status/context/decision/consequences structure used by existing ADRs (see 0006 for the pattern), covering: (1) the three invariants cited in the plan, (2) the three rejected alternatives the plan mentions, and (3) the positive rationale for the chosen approach (separate LLM sub-call for novelty vs deterministic fingerprint).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's description of the convergence-test seam: the test injects novelty scores by patching `_score_floor_novelty` (the `__init__.py` boundary function) via monkeypatch — not by feeding `FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})` through `score_novelty` — so future contributors patch the right seam.
findings.finding: The plan describes the convergence test mechanism as 'the convergence test drives `score_novelty` via a `FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})`', but the actual implementation in test_plan_review_rising_floor.py injects the novelty map by patching `_score_floor_novelty` directly via monkeypatch — not by feeding a FakeRunner structured output through `score_novelty`. An implementer reading the AC to add additional convergence tests would wire the wrong seam.
findings.location: Plan prerequisites section: 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via a FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via a FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})' with 'the convergence test injects the novelty map by patching _score_floor_novelty (the plan_review.__init__ boundary) via monkeypatch, keeping it offline with no LLM call'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's capability claim: confirm whether the convergence FakeRunner test uses `FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})` or a direct `run_chunk` injection, and update the Prerequisites section (and the forthcoming ADR 0007) to accurately describe the actual seam used.
findings.finding: The plan states 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via a FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})' but the actual delivered tests (test_plan_review_novelty.py:92–153) inject a run_chunk callable directly into verify.score_novelty — not through FakeRunner.structured at all. This is an unverified/imprecise capability assertion: FakeRunner.structured routes through _findings.validate_structured against an output_schema, which is a different seam than the direct run_chunk injection the tests actually use. The ADR and docs/plan-review-gate.md section should accurately describe the seam used, or the discrepancy should be resolved.
findings.location: Plan body — Prerequisites section: 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via a FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via a FakeRunner(structured={"novelties": ...})' with 'the convergence test drives score_novelty via an injected run_chunk callable (see test_plan_review_novelty.py:score_novelty_happy_path), which is the offline seam for the novelty sub-call — distinct from FakeRunner.structured which is used for full operation-level structured output'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
